qualifying_org_definition: Unpaid work at any organization or entity where the activity benefits the community or a member of the community not residing with the individual; Ohio Admin Code 5101:4-3-13 does not restrict to 501(c)(3) or specify org type — any community-benefiting unpaid work qualifies.
hours_formula_detail: 20 hours/week or 80 hours averaged monthly; explicitly stated in JFS 07410 form header and confirmed in Ohio Admin Code 5101:4-3-13.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Ohio Admin Code 5101:4-3-13 defines qualifying unpaid work solely as 'any activity where the individual performs something for no compensation and that benefits the community or a member of the community that does not reside with the individual.' No in-state requirement, registration requirement, or location restriction on the certifying organization is stated anywhere in the rule or on JFS 07410. Federal default (any qualifying nonprofit) applies.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. Ohio HB96 (signed June 30, 2025) prohibits ODJFS from seeking ABAWD waivers; the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (signed July 4, 2025) eliminated the waiver authority nationwide. No active Ohio county or regional waivers remain as of 2026.
submission_deadline: At certification/recertification and at any change in work nature (hours, location, entity). If hours drop below 20/week or 80/month, recipient must notify county JFS within 10 days following the month in which the change occurred (per JFS 07410 form instructions).
notes:
Ohio uses JFS 07410 (ABA Verification of Unpaid Hours) — a three-section self-reported form (participant info, activity contact info, unpaid activity info) submitted to the local county JFS at certification/recertification. Ohio Admin Code 5101:4-3-13 and 5101:4-2-09 contain no mention of MOU, EDP, provider registration, or county pre-approval as prerequisites; volunteer work at any community-benefiting organization appears to qualify. Confidence is medium rather than high because (a) the JFS 07410 PDF is encrypted and its exact org-signature requirement could not be confirmed, and (b) Ohio is county-administered across 88 counties and county-level operational variation is plausible even where state policy is clean. Ohio HB96 (signed June 30, 2025) bars the state from seeking ABAWD waivers, so ABAWD is fully enforced as of 2026.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted adversarial searches to attempt to refute the Tier 1 classification. Searched for: (1) Ohio SNAP work registration/intake enrollment requirements; (2) Ohio SNAP E&T State Plan FY2026 volunteer track; (3) Ohio SNAP approved volunteer site lists and county MOU requirements; (4) legal-aid and advocacy-org explainers on ABAWD community service verification. Key findings: (a) Ohio Admin Code 5101:4-3-13 was fetched directly from codes.ohio.gov and confirmed in plain language that no site pre-approval, no Employment Development Plan, no approved site list, and no MOU between county and nonprofit is required — the definition of qualifying unpaid work is broad ('any activity where the individual performs something for no compensation and that benefits the community or a member of the community that does not reside with the individual'); (b) JFS 07410 confirmed as the sole verification instrument — self-reported by the participant with activity contact info and unpaid activity details; (c) county-level sources reviewed (Union County, Licking County E&T Plan, Franklin County E&T Plan, Legal Aid Society of Cleveland) found no county-imposed gatekeeping that contradicts the state rule; (d) no approved volunteer site list or pre-registration requirement was found from any source; (e) remote_stance updated from 'unknown' to 'silent' — the rule contains no physical-location requirement, but also does not explicitly address remote/online work; (f) the county_variation_flag remains true given 88 counties with independent operational discretion, but the state rule provides a clean baseline. The Tier 1 classification survives adversarial scrutiny. Confidence raised from 'medium' to 'high' because the governing regulation was directly confirmed from the official source with explicit language ruling out the key prestep risk factors, and no contrary evidence was found at the county level. Note: Ohio HB96 (signed June 30, 2025) and the federal One Big Beautiful Bill Act (signed July 4, 2025) both tighten ABAWD enforcement — the work requirement is firmly in place and expanded to ages 18-64; this strengthens the relevance of the volunteer lane, not a barrier to it.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: confirmed federal_80 (20 hrs/week / 80 hrs/month) directly from JFS 07410 form text and Ohio Admin Code 5101:4-3-13 — Ohio follows the federal default, not the workfare allotment formula. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: silent — the qualifying-org definition in Admin Code 5101:4-3-13 contains zero location or registration requirements; no in-state restriction was found in any source; federal default (any qualifying nonprofit) applies; Tended as a CA-based 501(c)(3) should qualify operationally. (3) Geographic waivers: none as of 2026 — Ohio HB96 (signed June 30, 2025) bars ODJFS from seeking ABAWD waivers; OBBBA eliminated federal waiver authority nationwide; confirmed no active Ohio waivers remain. (4) Submission UX: JFS 07410 form explicitly lists mail, in-person (county JFS office), and fax as the three submission methods — no online portal or mobile upload is mentioned on the form. Reporting cadence is at_recertification (plus at any change in work hours/location/entity). The 10-day notification deadline applies when hours fall below threshold, not for routine monthly reporting. (5) Form fields: JFS 07410 was successfully extracted via pypdf; confirmed fields: Section 1 (recipient name, case number, address, phone); Section 2 (supervisor/contact name, phone, address, email, agency/org name, position/title); Section 3 (start date, expected end date, task description, weekly/total monthly hours schedule grid); signature of person completing form (org contact) + participant signature. No notarization required. EIN field not present.
